perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Compensation. The Company shall pay Consultant a flat fee consulting rate of $18,000 per month.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Compensation. The Company shall pay Consultant a flat fee consulting rate of $18,000 per month, contingent on the Consultant achieving a mutually agreed upon performance target.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'contingent on the Consultant achieving a mutually agreed upon performance target', we are introducing ambiguity into the compensation terms. The original text states a flat fee, while the modification suggests the fee is dependent on performance. This is conflicting and can be challenged. This contradicts the earlier statement of 'flat fee consulting rate'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Compensation. The Company shall pay Consultant a flat fee consulting rate of $18,000 per month, contingent on the Consultant achieving a mutually agreed upon performance target.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 'flat fee consulting rate,' implying a fixed and guaranteed payment. The changed text introduces a contingency based on 'achieving a mutually agreed upon performance target.' This directly contradicts the concept of a flat fee, as the payment is no longer guaranteed but dependent on performance. The contradicted text, in this case the changed text itself, highlights this by stating that the compensation is contingent on meeting a performance target, which is directly contradictory to the idea of a flat fee arrangement. This ambiguity and conflicting condition makes it difficult to enforce the 'flat fee' as originally intended, creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall be the sooner of six (6) months from the Effective Date, or replacement of this Agreement with a subsequent agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall be six (6) months from the Effective Date. The company may replace this agreement with a subsequent agreement at any time before the term is over, but the consultant cannot ask for a new agreement during the current term.
perturbation.explanation: The original states that the contract term is whichever is *sooner*, 6 months, or replacement of the contract. The changed text makes it a fixed 6-month term with the *option* for the company to replace the agreement, not necessarily ending the original term, but it also denies the consultant from requesting a new agreement during the current term, which is contradictory. This creates a contradiction with the second option of the original, which implies the agreement ends when a new one replaces it. Now it's ambiguous. This in-text contradiction means the 'Term' is conflicting and undefined, creating problems for enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall be six (6) months from the Effective Date. The company may replace this agreement with a subsequent agreement at any time before the term is over, but the consultant cannot ask for a new agreement during the current term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a fixed 6-month term while denying the consultant the ability to request a new agreement. This directly contradicts the original agreement, which allows for the term to end sooner if replaced by a subsequent agreement, thereby creating ambiguity around the agreement's termination, making the enforcement of the 'Term' undefined, and fulfilling the condition for an Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.6. Modification, Waiver. No modification of or amendment to this Agreement, nor any waiver of any rights under this Agreement, will be effective unless in a writing signed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.6. Modification, Waiver. No modification of or amendment to this Agreement will be effective unless approved verbally by the CEO, unless it involves financial obligations, in which case writing is required, nor any waiver of any rights under this Agreement, will be effective unless in a writing signed by the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that modifications can be made verbally by the CEO, except for financial obligations, we're contradicting the initial statement that modifications must be in writing. This introduces an ambiguity as to the valid method of modification, potentially leading to disputes over verbal agreements versus the written terms. This verbal exception is an in-text contradiction that makes the agreement difficult to enforce, due to contradicting statements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.6. Modification, Waiver. No modification of or amendment to this Agreement will be effective unless approved verbally by the CEO, unless it involves financial obligations, in which case writing is required, nor any waiver of any rights under this Agreement, will be effective unless in a writing signed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing verbal modifications approved by the CEO, except for financial obligations, which conflicts with the original requirement that all modifications be in writing. This creates an ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the acceptable method for modifying the agreement, thus the original statement in 12.6 is directly contradicted. The introduction of this ambiguity makes the agreement difficult to enforce due to conflicting statements regarding verbal modifications. Thus, adhering to Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
